summary: The Gradle build failed because one unit test failed: DbVersionTest.testCollectionVersion asserted that the owner version should not be incremented but it was.
root_cause: Test 'DbVersionTest > testCollectionVersion(SessionFactoryScope)' failed with an assertion: 'owner version not incremented ==> expected: <false> but was: <true>'.

evidence.reason: Shows which test failed and that it failed during Gradle test execution.

evidence.quote: org.opentest4j.AssertionFailedError: owner version not incremented ==> expected: <false> but was: <true>
evidence.reason: The assertion message and mismatch indicate the test expectation (owner version not incremented) did not match actual behavior.
Confirms only a single test failure caused the build to fail.
Gradle task for running hibernate-core tests failed as a result of the failing test.

evidence.quote: There were failing tests. See the report at: file:///home/runner/work/hibernate-orm/hibernate-orm/hibernate-core/target/reports/tests/test/index.html
evidence.reason: Points to the test report containing details of the failure for investigation.

suggested_fix: Run the failing test locally and inspect the test and related versioning code: reproduce with Gradle (e.g. run the specific test in :hibernate-core), open the report at /home/runner/work/hibernate-orm/hibernate-orm/hibernate-core/target/reports/tests/test/index.html, and either fix the code so the owner version is not incremented or update the test expectation if the behavior change is intentional.
